title: For a Surgery Tomorrow
scripture_text: Fear not, for I am with you; be not dismayed, for I am your God; I will strengthen you, I will help you, I will uphold you with my righteous right hand.
reflection: You have done the work of showing up. The rest is in hands bigger than yours.
body:
Tomorrow morning you will wake up early, before the rest of the world, and you will drive to a building where people in scrubs will take care of you.

Tonight, set out your clothes. Set out your phone charger. Tell someone you love them. Eat what they told you to eat, do not eat what they told you not to eat. Do the small things. The small things are how you keep your hands in your own life.

The Lord will be in the operating room. Not as a surgeon. As the one who holds the surgeon's hands, who steadies, who keeps.

You will wake up. The worst part will be the wait. The wait is almost over.
prayer: Lord, hold the surgeon's hands. Be with the nurses. Watch over me. And let me wake up grateful. Amen.
